methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Mara is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Daniel Kofman and Sriram Raghu. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public defense-tech firms, including peers such as Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses a $300M total-raised proxy as a base and applies a 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implying a $750M valuation. The Peer-Median method examines five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $150M and a range of $60M to $500M. This approach implies a $150M valuation for Mara.

The two methods yield a wide spread, resulting in a triangulated range of $128M (low) to $862M (high), with a median of $450M. The substantial gap between methods reflects uncertainty in whether Mara's capital-raise trajectory more closely tracks early-stage funding patterns or reflects the higher valuations observed in Series A peers. The low-end anchor of $128M derives from conservative stage-adjusted peer scaling, while the high-end reflects the stage-progression multiplier applied to cumulative funding. Key uncertainties include the reliability of the $300M proxy figure and whether peer Series A valuations appropriately discount for Mara's earlier stage.
